description_long: This cabin features a prominent gable roof clad in red ceramic tiles, with a regular grid pattern that adds a traditional touch. The structure is primarily constructed from light-colored wood, visible on the walls and supporting beams beneath the roof overhangs. There is a single, square window on one side, framed by the wooden wall, providing a simple yet functional opening. The cabin's design is compact and utilitarian, with no visible doors or decorative elements, emphasizing practicality. The roof extends well beyond the walls, offering additional protection from the elements. The overall appearance suggests a rustic, utilitarian shelter suitable for parks or residential backyards.
description: A small, rustic wooden cabin with a red-tiled gable roof and a single square window, featuring extended eaves and simple construction.
description_view_0: The cabin is seen from the side, showcasing the full expanse of the red-tiled gable roof and a single square window centered on the wooden wall.
description_view_1: This view highlights the gable end of the roof, with visible wooden beams and the square window directly beneath the roof peak.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the cabin is visible, showing the same red-tiled roof and a partial view of the window, with more of the wooden wall exposed.
description_view_3: The other gable end is shown, revealing the supporting wooden beams and the absence of windows or doors on this side.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with red ceramic tiles and extended eaves
attribute_window_arrangement: single square window centered on one side, no visible grouping or pattern
attribute_door_placement: no exterior doors visible on any side
